info.title: NXSAND
info.author: Antoine BOucher
info.description: GPU falling-sand sandbox for Nintendo Sw
info.details: NXSand is a falling-sand sandbox game for Nintendo Switch homebrew. Paint sand, water, lava, fire, smoke, plants, ice, oil, acid, glass, metal, wood, and more on a live pixel grid, then watch the materials fall, flow, burn, melt, dissolve, and react. The game supports Joy-Con and touchscreen controls, a material ring, brush size controls, quick save, and up to three saved worlds.\n\nThe simulation runs on the GPU using OpenGL ES, with a Margolus-style cellular automaton fast material updates. NXSand is designed mainly for Switch, with desktop builds available for testing and development.

changelog:
Initial v0.0.1 release.

Added Nintendo Switch homebrew build with NRO packaging.
Added GPU-based falling-sand simulation.
Added multiple materials and reactions, including sand, water, lava, fire, smoke, oil, acid, plant, ice, glass, wood, metal, gunpowder, and salt.
Added Joy-Con, touchscreen, and desktop input support.
Added material ring, brush controls, quick save, and save slots.
Added release packaging for Switch, Linux, and Windows.
